{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "UX Researcher",
  "description": "Moniepoint Incorporated is a global business payments and banking platform and recently became QED Investors&rsquo; first investment in Africa. We are the partner of choice for over 600,000 businesses of all sizes, powering the dreams of SMBs and providing them with equal access to the tools they need to grow and scale.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: UX Researcher

Job Location: Lagos

About the role


	As a &nbsp;UX researcher, you&rsquo;ll be the ears of our product team. You&rsquo;ll support our experienced researchers in uncovering the &quot;why&quot; behind user behaviours, helping us build products that truly make sense for people across Nigeria.
	This is a role for someone who is endlessly curious about how things work and wants to learn the craft of research from the ground up. You&rsquo;ll play a vital part in our mission to bring financial happiness to everyone by ensuring our products remain simple, helpful, and relatable.


Key Responsibilities


	Assist in planning and carrying out qualitative and quantitative research studies (from interviews to surveys).
	Take charge of research logistics, including finding and scheduling participants for studies.
	Help facilitate interviews and usability testing sessions, capturing the key insights that matter.
	Support the team in turning raw data into easy-to-understand reports and frameworks like journey maps and personas.
	Maintain our research repository, ensuring our findings are organised and easy for the whole company to find.
	Collaborate with designers and product managers to ensure research happens at every stage of the build.
	Keep your finger on the pulse of new UX tools and methods, sharing what you learn with the wider team.


Qualifications


	2&ndash;3 years of experience in UX research, market research, or a similar field where you&#39;ve had to understand human behaviour.
	A solid grasp of basic research methods (you know the difference between a survey and a deep-dive interview).
	Comfortable using digital tools and keen to master new ones (like Maze, Typeform, or Google Forms).
	Strong analytical thinking&mdash;you enjoy connecting the dots and finding patterns in information.


Preferred qualifications:


	An interest in fintech and how digital products are changing lives in Africa.
	Previous experience in a startup or fast-paced environment.
	A background in social sciences, psychology, or design.


About you


	You have a strong desire for mastery and aren&#39;t afraid to ask &quot;why&quot; until you truly understand.
	You look at problems from different angles and enjoy thinking of creative ways to get answers.
	You are approachable and friendly. You know how to make research participants feel at ease so they speak their truth.
	You take pride in being factual and organised. You ensure your notes are accurate and your data is clean.
	You&rsquo;re excited about the Nigerian market and want to discover the unique ways our users interact with money..


What success looks like


	Research projects run smoothly because the logistics and participant recruitment are handled proactively.
	You become proficient in conducting foundational research sessions independently within your first 6 months.
	The research repository is kept up-to-date, making it a &quot;source of truth&quot; for the product team.
	Stakeholders consistently praise the clarity and helpfulness of the research documentation you assist with.


What We Can Offer You


	Culture - We put our people first and prioritize the well-being of every team member. We&rsquo;ve built a company where all opinions carry weight and where all voices are heard. We value and respect each other and always look out for one another. Above all, we are human.
	Learning - We have a learning and development-focused environment with an emphasis on knowledge sharing, training, and regular internal technical talks.
	Compensation - You&rsquo;ll receive an attractive salary, pension, health insurance, annual bonus, plus other benefits.


Application Process


	Please submit a resume highlighting your research experience and outlining research methodologies, processes, and the impact of your work on user experience and business outcomes.
	Optionally, you may also include a PDF portfolio with case studies that further illustrate your research approach and results.
	While a portfolio is not required, it can provide additional insight into your end-to-end research thinking and influence on product direction.


Our hiring process:


	Resume review: We look for strong experience that showcases your research process, methodologies, and impact.
	Technical interview with a senior researcher: Deep dive into your experience, approach, and alignment with our mission.
	Case study presentation: Present a research project to a panel, demonstrating your problem-solving, research craft, and strategic thinking.
	Executive interview: Discussion with senior leadership about your growth potential, influence, and ability to drive user and business success.
	iscussion with senior leadership about your growth potential, influence, and ability to drive user and business success.
",

  "validThrough": "2026-08-07",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

  },

UX Researcher Job at Moniepoint




        
    

        
        Moniepoint Incorporated is a global business payments and banking platform and recently became QED Investors’ first investment in Africa. We are the partner of choice for over 600,000 businesses of all sizes, powering the dreams of SMBs and providing them with equal access to the tools they need to grow and scale.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: UX Researcher

Job Location: Lagos

About the role
     

            
    




	As a  UX researcher, you’ll be the ears of our product team. You’ll support our experienced researchers in uncovering the "why" behind user behaviours, helping us build products that truly make sense for people across Nigeria.
	This is a role for someone who is endlessly curious about how things work and wants to learn the craft of research from the ground up. You’ll play a vital part in our mission to bring financial happiness to everyone by ensuring our products remain simple, helpful, and relatable.


Key Responsibilities


	Assist in planning and carrying out qualitative and quantitative research studies (from interviews to surveys).
	Take charge of research logistics, including finding and scheduling participants for studies.
	Help facilitate interviews and usability testing sessions, capturing the key insights that matter.
	Support the team in turning raw data into easy-to-understand reports and frameworks like journey maps and personas.
	Maintain our research repository, ensuring our findings are organised and easy for the whole company to find.
	Collaborate with designers and product managers to ensure research happens at every stage of the build.
	Keep your finger on the pulse of new UX tools and methods, sharing what you learn with the wider team.


Qualifications


	2–3 years of experience in UX research, market research, or a similar field where you've had to understand human behaviour.
	A solid grasp of basic research methods (you know the difference between a survey and a deep-dive interview).
	Comfortable using digital tools and keen to master new ones (like Maze, Typeform, or Google Forms).
	Strong analytical thinking—you enjoy connecting the dots and finding patterns in information.


Preferred qualifications:


	An interest in fintech and how digital products are changing lives in Africa.
	Previous experience in a startup or fast-paced environment.
	A background in social sciences, psychology, or design.


About you


	You have a strong desire for mastery and aren't afraid to ask "why" until you truly understand.
	You look at problems from different angles and enjoy thinking of creative ways to get answers.
	You are approachable and friendly. You know how to make research participants feel at ease so they speak their truth.
	You take pride in being factual and organised. You ensure your notes are accurate and your data is clean.
	You’re excited about the Nigerian market and want to discover the unique ways our users interact with money..


What success looks like


	Research projects run smoothly because the logistics and participant recruitment are handled proactively.
	You become proficient in conducting foundational research sessions independently within your first 6 months.
	The research repository is kept up-to-date, making it a "source of truth" for the product team.
	Stakeholders consistently praise the clarity and helpfulness of the research documentation you assist with.


What We Can Offer You


	Culture - We put our people first and prioritize the well-being of every team member. We’ve built a company where all opinions carry weight and where all voices are heard. We value and respect each other and always look out for one another. Above all, we are human.
	Learning - We have a learning and development-focused environment with an emphasis on knowledge sharing, training, and regular internal technical talks.
	Compensation - You’ll receive an attractive salary, pension, health insurance, annual bonus, plus other benefits.


Application Process


	Please submit a resume highlighting your research experience and outlining research methodologies, processes, and the impact of your work on user experience and business outcomes.
	Optionally, you may also include a PDF portfolio with case studies that further illustrate your research approach and results.
	While a portfolio is not required, it can provide additional insight into your end-to-end research thinking and influence on product direction.


Our hiring process:


	Resume review: We look for strong experience that showcases your research process, methodologies, and impact.
	Technical interview with a senior researcher: Deep dive into your experience, approach, and alignment with our mission.
	Case study presentation: Present a research project to a panel, demonstrating your problem-solving, research craft, and strategic thinking.
	Executive interview: Discussion with senior leadership about your growth potential, influence, and ability to drive user and business success.
	iscussion with senior leadership about your growth potential, influence, and ability to drive user and business success.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

Executive Assitant - Special Duties Job at Advonics Services Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        Advonics Services Nigeria Limited, based in Lagos, provides cutting-edge security, safety surveillance, telecoms, and logistics services to both local and international clients. Known for quality and integrity, Advonics has over 15 years of experience and a reputation for innovative, dependable solutions.

The company has successfully partnered with government bodies, including projects with the Federal Ministry of Transportation and Ministry of Aviation. With a commitment to advanced technology, Advonics continually adapts to meet evolving client needs and maintain high service standards.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Executive Assitant - Special Duties
     

            
    



Job Location: Abuja & Lagos

Summary


	We're seeking talented, motivated, and forward-thinking professionals to join our fast-paced, high-performance team.


Requirement


	Interested candidates should possess relevant qualifications.


Why Work With Us?


	Work with visionary leaders and innovators
	Build a rewarding career with room for growth

title: Job vacancies at Luna Properties and Investment Limited
content:
Luna Properties and Investment Limited is a Nigerian real estate firm based in the Lekki-Epe axis of Lagos. They focus on selling, developing, and managing genuine and verifiable lands and luxury homes across Lagos, Abuja, and other states.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Videographer

Job Location: Lagos

Requirement
     

            
    




	Interested candidates should be creative and skilled in shooting and editing high-quality videos for promotions and brand storytelling.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note


	For more enquiries, call: 07025334773.
	Applicants should stay around Ajah or its environs.
	Join our growing real estate team and be part of a dynamic, professional and result-driven company.


 

 

2.) Graphic Designer

Job Location: Lagos

Requirement


	Interested candidates should be creative and skilled in designing engaging visuals for digital and print platforms.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note


	For more enquiries, call: 07025334773.
	Applicants should stay around Ajah or its environs.
	Join our growing real estate team and be part of a dynamic, professional and result-driven company.


 

 

3.) Personal Assistant

Job Location: Lagos

Requirements


	Interested candidates should be organized, proactive and detail-oriented individuals who can provide executive support and ensure smooth daily operations.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Personal Assistant" as the subject of the email.

Note


	For more enquiries, call: 07025334773.
	Applicants should stay around Ajah or its environs.
	Join our growing real estate team and be part of a dynamic, professional and result-driven company.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "Westfield Consulting Limited - Our client in the Financial Sector (Microfinance Bank) is looking for qualified and passionate candidates for the position below:

Job Position: Project Manager
Job Location: Ojodu Berger, Lagos&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Lead and deliver bank-wide projects that grow revenue, improve operations, ensure CBN / NDIC compliance, and scale financial inclusion products.


Job Description


	End-to-End Project Management: Initiate, plan, execute, monitor and close projects: new product launches, core banking upgrades, branch expansion, agency banking rollout, mobile app, financial inclusion programs.
	Product Rollouts: Lead launch of micro-loans, group lending, POS/Agent banking, savings products, USSD, mobile app. Work with Marketing and Risk.
	Core Banking Integration: Manage CBS upgrades, integration with NIBSS, CBN, NIMC, BVN, credit bureaus, payment gateways.
	Process Re-engineering: Map AS-IS processes, design TO-BE. Drive automation to reduce loan TAT
	Regulatory Projects: Drive CBN/NDIC compliance projects: AML/CFT upgrade
	Scope, Timeline &amp; Budget Control: Develop project charter, WBS, Gantt, risk register.
	Stakeholder Management: Align Business, IT, Risk, Compliance, Ops, Branches, and Vendors. Run steering committee meetings and status reports.
	Vendor &amp; Contract Management: Manage fintech vendors, consultants, contractors. Ensure SLAs and value-for-money.
	Post-Implementation Review: Track benefits 3-6 months after go-live: revenue, cost saving, customer uptake.
	Change Management: Train staff, develop SOPs, manage communication
	Data Protection, Cybersecurity framework implementation.


Job Requirements


	First Degree in any Discipline with a minimum of Second Class lower or HND with Upper Credit division.
	Minimum of five years&rsquo; experience with 3 years managing projects in a bank, fintech, or financial services. MFB experience is a strong plus.
	PMP certification is an added advantage.
	Attention to detail, accuracy, and precision are essential.
	Knowledge of CBN Microfinance Bank guidelines, AML/CFT, Agency Banking Framework, Financial Inclusion Strategy.
	Great knowledge of computers and Microsoft Office suites.
	Excellent communication and customer service skills
	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills


Salary
N250,000 (Monthly gross).

Method of Applictaion&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: victor.allu@westfield-consulting.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Project Manager Job at Westfield Consulting Limited




        
    

        
        Westfield Consulting Limited - Our client in the Financial Sector (Microfinance Bank) is looking for qualified and passionate candidates for the position below:

Job Position: Project Manager
Job Location: Ojodu Berger, Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Lead and deliver bank-wide projects that grow revenue, improve operations, ensure CBN / NDIC compliance, and scale financial inclusion products.


Job Description
     

            
    




	End-to-End Project Management: Initiate, plan, execute, monitor and close projects: new product launches, core banking upgrades, branch expansion, agency banking rollout, mobile app, financial inclusion programs.
	Product Rollouts: Lead launch of micro-loans, group lending, POS/Agent banking, savings products, USSD, mobile app. Work with Marketing and Risk.
	Core Banking Integration: Manage CBS upgrades, integration with NIBSS, CBN, NIMC, BVN, credit bureaus, payment gateways.
	Process Re-engineering: Map AS-IS processes, design TO-BE. Drive automation to reduce loan TAT
	Regulatory Projects: Drive CBN/NDIC compliance projects: AML/CFT upgrade
	Scope, Timeline & Budget Control: Develop project charter, WBS, Gantt, risk register.
	Stakeholder Management: Align Business, IT, Risk, Compliance, Ops, Branches, and Vendors. Run steering committee meetings and status reports.
	Vendor & Contract Management: Manage fintech vendors, consultants, contractors. Ensure SLAs and value-for-money.
	Post-Implementation Review: Track benefits 3-6 months after go-live: revenue, cost saving, customer uptake.
	Change Management: Train staff, develop SOPs, manage communication
	Data Protection, Cybersecurity framework implementation.


Job Requirements


	First Degree in any Discipline with a minimum of Second Class lower or HND with Upper Credit division.
	Minimum of five years’ experience with 3 years managing projects in a bank, fintech, or financial services. MFB experience is a strong plus.
	PMP certification is an added advantage.
	Attention to detail, accuracy, and precision are essential.
	Knowledge of CBN Microfinance Bank guidelines, AML/CFT, Agency Banking Framework, Financial Inclusion Strategy.
	Great knowledge of computers and Microsoft Office suites.
	Excellent communication and customer service skills
	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills


Salary
N250,000 (Monthly gross).

Method of Applictaion 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply
